problem_statement: The project needs a shell script that pulls the latest changes from the main public repository so that updates can be easily applied and observed. This fits the existing pattern of utility scripts in the `scripts/` directory.
implementation_plan: Create a new script `scripts/forge-update.sh` that fetches and pulls the latest changes from the main public remote (origin main).
Include safety checks: ensure the working directory is clean (no uncommitted changes) before pulling, and handle errors gracefully (e.g., network failure, merge conflicts).
Print clear status messages before and after the update (e.g., 'Fetching latest changes...', 'Update complete.' or 'Update failed.').
Make the script executable and follow the same conventions (shebang, style) as existing scripts like `scripts/forge-cleanup.sh` and `scripts/forge-create.sh`.
acceptance_criteria: A new file `scripts/forge-update.sh` exists and is executable.
Running the script fetches and pulls the latest changes from the default remote's main branch.
The script warns and exits gracefully if there are uncommitted local changes.
The script prints meaningful output on success and on failure.
The script follows the same shell scripting conventions as other scripts in `scripts/`.
